cues.caption: The vertical PVC tower in Almere — lettuce and radish, with a rain tarp on the left

cues.title: The three target windows
lines: pH — 5.8 to 6.2 sweet spot, 5.5 to 6.8 acceptable
EC — 850 to 1200 microsiemens vegetative, 1200+ for radish bulbs
Reservoir temperature — 18 to 22 degrees optimum

cues.caption: Roots take up nitrate and release hydroxide — so the reservoir drifts alkaline

cues.title: The drift rule
lines: Healthy plants eating nitrate → pH goes up
The cure is acid, almost never base
If pH falls on its own → root rot, not chemistry

cues.caption: A strong buffer absorbs acid before pH moves; with no buffer, one drop swings it hard

cues.title: The carbonate buffer chain
lines: CO₂ + H₂O ⇌ H₂CO₃ ⇌ H⁺ + HCO₃⁻
This pair sits at pH 5 to 7 — exactly your hydroponic range
Acid added → bicarbonate eats it → barely a pH change

cues.caption: The full Colombo Aquatest row at Ranzijn — KH, GH, Nitrite, pH, Ammonia, Nitrate, Phosphate
The Colombo Aquatest KH kit, opened — reagent bottle and titration tube
A Colombo pH test reading 6.5 — held against the white half of the card

cues.title: Your starter kit at Ranzijn
lines: Colombo Aquatest pH — 9 euros 99
Colombo Aquatest KH — 9 euros 99
Total — 20 euros, lifetime supply for a single tower

cues.title: KH benchmarks
lines: Rainwater — 0 to 1 °dKH (no buffer, easy to overshoot)
Target for hydroponics — 2 to 4 °dKH (gentle buffer)
Almere tap — measured 6 °dKH (moderate buffer)
Demi / RO water — 0 °dKH

cues.title: EC versus the specific-ion kits
lines: EC meter — one bulk number: how much nutrient is in there at all
Nitrate kit — is the nitrogen specifically running low
Nitrite and Ammonia near zero — the roots are healthy

cues.caption: JBL ProAquatest Easy 7-in-1 — tube with a printed colour chart
UNITEST pH 1–11 wide-range strips — useless in the 6–7.5 range that matters
SuperFish pH Min — 1 L pond/aquarium pH down, €14.50
SuperFish pH Min back panel — Dutch pond-dosing instructions, active ingredient unclear
BSI pH Down — €3.46 for 1 L at Gamma, sodium-bisulfate based

cues.title: Why BSI pH Down beats citric acid here
lines: Sulfate is a plant nutrient, not a pollutant
No organic carbon → no microbes → no pH bounce
3 euros 46 a litre — cheaper per dose, and you know what is in it

cues.title: Dosing the 4-litre tower on tap water
lines: Fresh mix, pH 7.5 → 6.0 — about 5 to 6 mL of BSI, dosed direct
Daily top-up, pH ~7 → 6.5 — about 3 mL, dosed direct
Measure with a syringe, add, stir, wait, re-test

cues.caption: A repurposed demi-water bottle — the 1:20 working solution, kept only for rainwater
Colombo FloraGrow Pro — 500 mL planted-aquarium fertiliser, €19.95

cues.title: Pushing K and P for radish bulbing
lines: Run A+B a little stronger — crude, but gets you most of the way
Mono-potassium phosphate, MKP — the classic cheap bloom salt, adds both P and K
A 3-part nutrient, Grow Micro Bloom — you mix the ratio yourself

cues.caption: Dissolved oxygen falls as water warms; plant uptake rises — the sweet spot is the overlap
SuperFish Eco Heater 50W — a 17 cm submersible aquarium heater, rated 20–50 L, €15
The heater submerged in the 4 L reservoir at night, running flat out
The heater in the reservoir, tower above — heat loss to the night air is the real opponent

cues.title: Minimum kit — about 23 euros
lines: Colombo Aquatest pH drop kit — 9 euros 99
Colombo Aquatest KH drop kit — 9 euros 99
BSI pH Down from Gamma — 3 euros 46

cues.title: Recommended kit — 35 to 45 euros
lines: Above plus a TDS or EC meter from Action or Bol — 10 to 15 euros
A cheap aquarium thermometer — 3 to 5 euros

cues.title: Serious kit — 50 to 65 euros
lines: Above plus a Colombo Nitrate kit to cross-check the meter — 15 euros
SuperFish Eco Heater 50W — 15 euros

cues.title: Fresh reservoir with rainwater
lines: 1 — Fill reservoir with rainwater
2 — Add A+B nutrients to EC 900 to 1200
3 — Stir, wait 10 minutes
4 — Test pH — expect 5.8 to 6.3
5 — If dosing is needed, use the 1:20 BSI solution, not neat
6 — Recheck every 2 to 3 days

cues.title: Fresh reservoir with tap water
lines: 1 — Fill with Almere tap water
2 — Add A+B to EC 900 to 1200
3 — Stir, wait 10 minutes
4 — Test pH — expect about 7.5
5 — Dose BSI pH Down direct with a syringe, ~5 to 6 mL
6 — Stir, wait, re-test — land at pH 6.0
7 — Recheck every few days; top up ~3 mL as it drifts back up
